steps.title: Identify the key mechanism
steps.content: The explanation claims Mexican art objects were traded to Missouri and influenced a local artist. To support this, we need evidence that Mexican art was actually present in the region during the Mississippian period.

steps.title: Match to the evidence
steps.content:
Choice C provides exactly this evidence: an ancient Mexican art object dated to the Mississippian period, found near where the Fairfield Gorget was discovered, proves Mexican art was in the region and accessible to local artisans.

The other choices are irrelevant—they address the gorget's dating, jaguar habitat, or modern influences rather than ancient trade.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
